mission.title: Install and Configure Argo CD Operator
mission.description: The Argo CD Operator is a Kubernetes operator designed to manage Argo CD clusters efficiently. It simplifies the deployment and management of Argo CD instances, making it ideal for GitOps workflows. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes operators and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Argo CD Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Argo CD Operator Helm repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add argocd-operator https://argoproj.github.io/argo-helm

```

> This is the official repository for the Argo CD Operator.

steps.title: Install the Argo CD Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Argo CD Operator in the 'argocd' namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install argocd-operator argocd-operator/argocd-operator --namespace argocd --create-namespace --version 0.17.0

```

> Make sure to specify the correct version.

steps.title: Create the CatalogSource
steps.description:
This step creates a CatalogSource resource that points to the Argo CD Operator image.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: CatalogSource
metadata:
  name: argocd-catalog
spec:
  sourceType: grpc
  image: quay.io/argoprojlabs/argocd-operator-registry@sha256:f0d2bb73e8b9d0561c931b2f2afc81cf28ca5711c476b02ac1887770e799ab92
  displayName: Argo CD Operators
  publisher: Argo CD Community
EOF

```

> This YAML creates the CatalogSource for the operator.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: CatalogSource
metadata:
  name: argocd-catalog
spec:
  sourceType: grpc
  image: quay.io/argoprojlabs/argocd-operator-registry@sha256:f0d2bb73e8b9d0561c931b2f2afc81cf28ca5711c476b02ac1887770e799ab92
  displayName: Argo CD Operators
  publisher: Argo CD Community
EOF

steps.title: Create the OperatorGroup
steps.description:
This step creates an OperatorGroup resource to manage the Argo CD Operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1
kind: OperatorGroup
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
EOF

```

> This YAML creates the OperatorGroup for the operator.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1
kind: OperatorGroup
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
EOF

steps.title: Create the Subscription
steps.description:
This step creates a Subscription resource to subscribe to the Argo CD Operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: Subscription
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
spec:
  channel: alpha
  name: argocd-operator
  source: argocd-catalog
  sourceNamespace: olm
EOF

```

> This YAML creates the Subscription for the operator.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: Subscription
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
spec:
  channel: alpha
  name: argocd-operator
  source: argocd-catalog
  sourceNamespace: olm
EOF

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This command checks if the Argo CD Operator is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n argocd

```

> You should see the Argo CD Operator pod running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Argo CD Operator
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Argo CD Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may result in data loss if any Argo CD instances are managed by the operator.

```bash

helm uninstall argocd-operator --namespace argocd

kubectl delete namespace argocd

```

> This will remove the operator.
> This will delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Argo CD Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important configurations. The upgrade process involves uninstalling the current version and installing the new version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n argocd -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade argocd-operator argocd-operator/argocd-operator --namespace argocd --version 0.17.0

kubectl get pods -n argocd

```

> Drain the pods before upgrade.
> This command upgrades the operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n argocd -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Subscription" in version "operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The OpenShift Operator Lifecycle Manager (OLM) is not installed or not running.

**Fix:**
Install OLM by following the official documentation.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create CatalogSource: catalog source already exists'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CatalogSource with the same name already exists in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Delete the existing CatalogSource using 'kubectl delete catalogsource argocd-catalog -n olm' before reapplying.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Argo CD Operator pod may be misconfigured or unable to start due to missing permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n argocd' and ensure all required permissions are granted.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "OperatorGroup" in version "operators.coreos.com/v1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The OLM is not installed or the API version is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure OLM is installed and running, and verify the API version.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for multi-cluster management and improved error handling in the operator.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Argo CD Operator pod running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n argocd'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
